questions.question_text: Explain the concept of Net Present Value and its importance in financial decision making? CO - 2

What is the Internal Rate of Return ? Discuss its significance and how it is used to evaluate investment projects. Calculate the IRR for the following cash flow series Initial Investment = Rs. 6,00,000 Cash flow: Year 1 = Rs. 1,50,000 Year 2 = Rs. 2,00,000 Year 3 = Rs. 2,50,000 Year 4 = Rs. 3,00,000 CO - 1
What is Sensitivity analysis in financial modeling? How does it help business understand the impact of changes in key assumptions on their financial results? CO - 3

A Company has the following projections for its next 3 years: Year 1 Revenue = Rs. 20,00,000 Operating Expenses = Rs. 10,00,000 Depreciation = Rs. 1,00,000 Year 2 Revenue = Rs. 2,50,000 Operating Expenses = Rs. 12,00,000 CO - 1 2 Depreciation = Rs. 1,20,000 Year 3 Revenue = Rs. 30,00,000 Operating Expenses = Rs. 15,00,000 Depreciation = Rs. 1,50,000 Calculate the free cash flow for each year.

A portfolio consists of two assets, A & B with the following information: Expected Return of Asset A = 9% Expected Return of Asset = 8% Standard Deviation of Asset A = 5% Standard Deviation of Asset B = 10% Correlation Coefficient between Asset A & Asset B = 0.4 Calculate the expected return and standard deviation of a portfolio with 40% weight in Asset A and 60% weight in Asset B. CO - 2
Why is diversification important in a portfolio? What are the benefits of adding more assets with low correlation to a portfolio? CO - 2
What are Treasury Notes? How are they used to reflect the term structure of interest rates? CO – 4

A Bond pays a fixed coupon of Rs.3500 every year and has a face value of Rs.70,000. The bond matures in 5 years, the current market interest rate is 6%. What is the price of the bond? What would happen to the bond price if the interest rate increased to 8%? CO - 4
